description: TDD: Write failing tests for expanded pipeline coverage, multi-environment support, and CDK Pipeline construct, then implement minimal code to make them pass. Update ARCHITECTURE.md with deployment structure.
steps: 1a. Create tests/unit/test_multi_environment.py with tests that verify environment-specific behavior via CDK context: (1) log retention differs by environment (7 days dev, 30 days stage, 90 days prod), (2) removal policy is RETAIN for prod, DESTROY otherwise, (3) stack ID includes environment name, (4) DynamoDB billing mode changes per env context. These tests should use a parameterized conftest fixture that passes context {'environment': 'dev|stage|prod'} to the App.

1b. Create tests/unit/test_pipeline_construct.py with tests that verify: (1) a CDK Pipeline construct (aws_cdk.pipelines.CodePipeline) is synthesized in a pipeline stack, (2) the pipeline has at least one stage (application stage), (3) the application stage produces the CdkBaseStack resources. Import a new PipelineStack from cdk_base/pipeline_stack.py.

1c. Expand tests/unit/test_pipeline_e2e.py with new tests: (1) snapshot test of the full template (template_snapshot using template.to_json()), (2) test that ProcessAudio Lambda payload contains only bucket and object fields (scoped input), (3) test that the failure notification message includes a 'reason' field from validationError, (4) test error paths: WriteInitialRecord catch routes to Fail, ProcessAudio catch routes to UpdateStatusFailed, PollyTask catch routes to UpdateStatusFailed.

1d. Add tests to tests/unit/test_lambda_handler.py: (1) test handler returns valid=True with all required metadata fields for valid input, (2) test handler with nested path keys like 'folder/subfolder/file.wav' works correctly.
STEP 2 - Implement code to make tests pass:

2a. Modify cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py to read environment context from self.node.try_get_context('environment') with 'dev' as default. Use it to: (1) set log retention (7/30/90 days), (2) set removal policies (DESTROY for dev/stage, RETAIN for prod), (3) set DynamoDB billing mode (PAY_PER_REQUEST for dev/stage, PROVISIONED with auto-scaling for prod).

2b. Create cdk_base/pipeline_stack.py with a PipelineStack class that: (1) defines a CodePipeline using aws_cdk.pipelines, (2) adds a GitHub source (placeholder connection ARN), (3) adds a synth step, (4) adds an application stage containing CdkBaseStack.

2c. Update app.py to: (1) read environment context, (2) include environment in the stack ID (e.g., 'CdkBaseStack-dev'), (3) conditionally instantiate PipelineStack when context 'deploy_pipeline' is true.
2d. Update tests/conftest.py to add parameterized fixtures: (1) a fixture that creates an app with specific environment context, (2) a fixture for pipeline stack template.
3a. Add a 'Deployment Pipeline' section describing the CDK Pipelines construct, stages, and how environments are deployed.
3b. Update the Mermaid diagram to include a deployment pipeline flow (Source -> Synth -> Deploy Dev -> Deploy Stage -> Deploy Prod).
3c. Expand the 'Multi-Environment Support' section with specifics of what changes per environment (log retention, removal policies, billing mode) and how to deploy with context.
4a. Add cdk synth commands with different environment contexts to .github/workflows/ci.yml to verify multi-env synthesis works.
acceptance_criteria: All new tests pass: pytest tests/ exits with code 0
ARCHITECTURE.md has a Deployment Pipeline section with Mermaid diagram
Multi-environment behavior is controlled by CDK context 'environment' value
PipelineStack exists in cdk_base/pipeline_stack.py and can be synthesized
CI workflow includes multi-environment synth validation
verification: pytest tests/ -v exits with code 0 and shows all new tests passing
cdk synth -c environment=dev produces valid template
cdk synth -c environment=prod produces valid template with RETAIN removal policies
python -c "from cdk_base.pipeline_stack import PipelineStack" succeeds (import check)
findings: All 119 tests pass with 100% coverage. CDK synth works for dev, stage, and prod environments. The template fixture remains backward-compatible (defaults to dev). Prod correctly applies RETAIN removal policies. Lambda function count is 3 (includes 2 auto-delete custom resource Lambdas from S3 buckets in dev mode). PipelineStack imports and synthesizes successfully. Python 3.9 is used in this sandbox.
